questoes.pergunta: Change to passive: 'Shakespeare wrote Hamlet.'
opcoes: a) Hamlet was wrote by Shakespeare.
b) Hamlet was written by Shakespeare.
d) Hamlet is written by Shakespeare.

questoes.pergunta: Complete: 'English _____ all over the world.'
questoes.explicacao: Present simple passive: is/are + past participle.

questoes.pergunta: Change to passive: 'They are building a new hospital.'
opcoes: a) A new hospital is being built.
b) A new hospital is built.
c) A new hospital is building.
d) A new hospital being built.
questoes.explicacao: Present continuous passive: is/are + being + past participle.

questoes.pergunta: Complete: 'The letter _____ yesterday.'
questoes.explicacao: Past simple passive: was/were + past participle.

questoes.pergunta: Change to passive: 'Someone has stolen my car.'
opcoes: a) My car has been stolen.
d) My car have been stolen.
questoes.explicacao: Present perfect passive: has/have + been + past participle.

questoes.pergunta: Complete: 'The room _____ every day.'
Change to passive: 'They will finish the work tomorrow.'
opcoes: a) The work will be finished tomorrow.
b) The work will finish tomorrow.
c) The work is finished tomorrow.
d) The work will be finish tomorrow.
questoes.explicacao: Future passive: will + be + past participle.

questoes.pergunta: Complete: 'When I arrived, the food _____ already cooked.'
questoes.explicacao: Past perfect passive: had been + past participle.

questoes.pergunta: Change to passive: 'They are going to open a new store.'
opcoes: a) A new store is going to be opened.
b) A new store is going to open.
c) A new store will be opened.
d) A new store is opened.
questoes.explicacao: Future with 'going to' passive: is going to be + past participle.

questoes.pergunta: Complete: 'This house _____ in 1990.'
questoes.explicacao: Past simple passive with specific time.

questoes.pergunta: Change to passive: 'People speak Portuguese in Brazil.'
opcoes: a) Portuguese is spoken in Brazil.
b) Portuguese are spoken in Brazil.
d) Portuguese is speak in Brazil.

questoes.pergunta: Complete: 'The problem _____ now.'
questoes.explicacao: Present continuous passive: is being discussed.

questoes.pergunta: Change to passive: 'They had finished the project.'
opcoes: a) The project had been finished.
c) The project has been finished.

questoes.pergunta: Complete: 'The cake _____ by my mother.'
Change to passive: 'They will have completed the bridge by next year.'
opcoes: a) The bridge will have been completed by next year.
b) The bridge will be completed by next year.
c) The bridge will have completed by next year.
d) The bridge is completed by next year.
questoes.explicacao: Future perfect passive: will have been + past participle.

questoes.pergunta: Complete: 'This song _____ on the radio a lot.'
Change to passive: 'They were painting the house when I arrived.'
opcoes: a) The house was being painted when I arrived.
b) The house was painted when I arrived.
c) The house was painting when I arrived.
d) The house is being painted when I arrived.
questoes.explicacao: Past continuous passive: was/were being + past participle.

questoes.pergunta: Complete: 'The letter _____ written yet.'
Change to passive: 'Someone should clean this room.'
opcoes: a) This room should be cleaned.
c) This room is should be cleaned.
d) This room should been cleaned.
questoes.explicacao: Modal passive: modal + be + past participle.

questoes.pergunta: Complete: 'The car _____ serviced regularly.'
Change to passive: 'They are going to invite everyone.'
opcoes: a) Everyone is going to be invited.
b) Everyone is going to invite.

questoes.pergunta: Complete: 'I hope the package _____ by Friday.'
Change to passive: 'They have offered him a new job.'
opcoes: a) He has been offered a new job.
b) A new job has been offered to him.
d) He has offered a new job.
questoes.explicacao: Verbs with two objects can have two passives.

questoes.pergunta: Complete: 'The results _____ announced tomorrow.'
Change to passive: 'They were interviewing her for the job.'
opcoes: a) She was being interviewed for the job.
b) She was interviewed for the job.
c) She is being interviewed for the job.
d) She had been interviewed for the job.

questoes.pergunta: Complete: 'The old building _____ down next year.'
Change to passive: 'People don't use this road very often.'
opcoes: a) This road isn't used very often.
b) This road doesn't used very often.
c) This road isn't use very often.
d) This road is not use very often.

questoes.pergunta: Complete: 'By the time we arrived, the decision _____ made.'
Change to passive: 'You must pay the bill on time.'
opcoes: a) The bill must be paid on time.
b) The bill must pay on time.
c) The bill is must paid on time.
d) The bill must been paid on time.

questoes.pergunta: Complete: 'The film _____ based on a true story.'
opcoes: d) all are possible depending on context
questoes.explicacao: Could be present or past. Most common: is based (present passive).

questoes.pergunta: Change to passive: 'They are going to build a new airport.'
opcoes: a) A new airport is going to be built.
b) A new airport is going to build.
c) A new airport will be built.
d) A new airport is built.

questoes.pergunta: Complete: 'The documents _____ signed yet.'
Change to passive: 'Someone will meet you at the airport.'
opcoes: a) You will be met at the airport.
b) You will meet at the airport.
c) You will been met at the airport.
d) You are met at the airport.

questoes.pergunta: Complete: 'This room _____ every day.'
Change to passive: 'They had already discussed the issue.'
opcoes: a) The issue had already been discussed.
b) The issue was already discussed.
c) The issue has already been discussed.
d) The issue had already discussed.

questoes.pergunta: Complete: 'The bridge _____ at the moment.'
Change to passive: 'They should have informed us earlier.'
opcoes: a) We should have been informed earlier.
b) We should be informed earlier.
c) We should have informed earlier.
d) We should been informed earlier.
questoes.explicacao: Modal perfect passive: should have been + past participle.

questoes.pergunta: Complete: 'The game _____ postponed due to rain.'
questoes.explicacao: Depends on context. Most common: was postponed.

questoes.pergunta: Change to passive: 'People consider him a genius.'
opcoes: a) He is considered a genius.
b) He is being considered a genius.
c) He was considered a genius.
d) He has been considered a genius.

questoes.pergunta: Complete: 'The report _____ by the end of the day.'
Change to passive: 'They are watching the suspect.'
opcoes: a) The suspect is being watched.
c) The suspect was being watched.
d) The suspect has been watched.

questoes.pergunta: Complete: 'The cake _____ into three layers.'
Change to passive: 'They had not invited her.'
opcoes: a) She had not been invited.
c) She has not been invited.

questoes.pergunta: Complete: 'The results _____ until next week.'
Change to passive: 'Someone must have taken it.'
opcoes: a) It must have been taken.

questoes.pergunta: Complete: 'The children _____ to school by bus.'
Change to passive: 'They will have finished the project by Monday.'
opcoes: a) The project will have been finished by Monday.
b) The project will be finished by Monday.
c) The project will have finished by Monday.
d) The project is finished by Monday.

questoes.pergunta: Complete: 'The documents _____ by the manager.'
Change to passive: 'They were cleaning the house when I arrived.'
opcoes: a) The house was being cleaned when I arrived.
b) The house was cleaned when I arrived.
c) The house was cleaning when I arrived.
d) The house had been cleaned when I arrived.

questoes.pergunta: Complete: 'I hope the problem _____ soon.'
